faq.question: What is a demand letter?
faq.answer: A demand letter is a formal written notice requesting payment of a debt or resolution of a dispute. It puts the recipient on notice that you intend to take legal action if the matter is not resolved, and it creates a paper trail for court.

faq.question: Do I need a lawyer to send a demand letter?
faq.answer: No, you can write and send your own demand letter. However, a letter from an attorney often carries more weight. For large amounts or complex disputes, consulting an attorney is recommended.

faq.question: How should I send a demand letter?
faq.answer: Send via certified mail with return receipt requested through USPS. This provides proof that the letter was delivered. Keep a copy for your records. Some people also send via email as a backup.

faq.question: What happens if they ignore my demand letter?
faq.answer: If the recipient does not respond or pay, you can file a lawsuit. For amounts under your state's small claims limit (typically $5,000-$10,000), you can file in small claims court without an attorney. The demand letter becomes evidence of your good-faith effort to resolve the matter.

sections.title: What Is the Demand Letter Generator?
sections.body: The Demand Letter Generator helps you create professional legal documents quickly and accurately without expensive attorney fees. Instead of guessing or spending hours on manual calculations, get accurate results in seconds. Enter your details above and let the calculator do the work.

sections.title: Why Use a Document Generator?
sections.body: Legal documents drafted incorrectly can be unenforceable or create liability you did not intend. While this generator creates professionally structured documents based on widely accepted templates, complex situations may still warrant review by a licensed attorney. Use this as a starting point to save time and money on standard legal paperwork.
